body.title: comms://or-chat/* — stateful multi-turn LLM channels with UC-managed scrollback
body.result: OrChatTransport at lab/utility_closet/transports/or_chat.py. Layers on InferenceTransport — chat/user msg → assemble scrollback prompt → gateway → chat/assistant emit. System prompt pinned; oldest-first char-budget trimming. URI: comms://or-chat/<purpose>/<session-id>. 11 tests pass. Follow-ups in commit msg: token-aware trim, structured chat API to gateway, session lifecycle verbs, multi-participant. Commit 98ca45ca.
body.description:
Make it possible to say 'open a channel to <OR model>' and carry on a multi-turn conversation without managing context. The transcript lives in the UC comm unit; each turn appends to it; UC trims to the model's context window when assembling the next prompt. Uhura-at-comms framing — one verb (open channel), the plumbing is hidden. Each channel is its own comm unit with its own short-term memory — separate from Igor's TWM.

## Motivation
Akien: 'open a channel to cc and ask him that question / open a channel with <model on OR> / open a channel to leah via google talk.' CC and Leah manage their own context; a raw OR model doesn't, so the channel itself has to carry the scrollback. Same user-facing verb across transports is the whole point.

## Design
- comms://or-chat/<model>/<session-id>
- Each session has a conversation store inside the comm unit (likely channel_messages keyed by channel name).
- On user turn: append message → pull recent scrollback trimmed to model window → send via inference transport → append response.
- System prompt lives in session metadata (settable at open-channel time).
- Session eviction: idle timeout + explicit close; persistence across UC restart expected.

## Differentiated from T-uc-inference-channel
- That one is stateless request/response RPC — one call, one answer, no memory.
- This one is stateful multi-turn — the channel remembers prior turns and carries them forward.
- This layers on top of that: each turn of a chat channel is an inference-channel RPC.

## Scope
- New transport: lab/utility_closet/transports/or_chat.py
- Scrollback store in UC (reuse channel_messages or new chat_sessions table — design call during sprint)
- API shape: open(model, system_prompt?) → session_id; send(session_id, text) → reply; close(session_id)
- Context-window trim policy: token-count-aware, drop-oldest-first, keep system prompt pinned

## Depends on
- T-uc-comms-module (done)
- T-uc-inference-channel (pending — this layers on top, or can ship in parallel using a stub inference call)

## Biomimetic note
The channel has its own short-term memory of the chat — separate from Igor's TWM. Each Uhura channel is a thin cortex of its own.
